user.title: Anker X1 SmartGrid
user.description: Pick your Anker SOLIX X1 and the core sensors the planner reads. Everything else gets sensible defaults and can be tuned later via Configure.

data_description.anker_device: Battery capacity and the SoC, battery-power, setpoint, work-mode and Modbus-control entities are derived from this device automatically.
data_description.ent_price: Sensor reporting the current electricity import tariff in €/kWh (e.g. Zonneplan). Drives all charge and export planning.
data_description.ent_weather_forecast: Weather entity providing the hourly forecast used by the load model.
data_description.forecast_service: Pick one or more forecast integrations. Their PV sensors are added together (summed), so select multiple only when they cover different arrays — otherwise PV reads high.

error.anker_roles_missing: The selected device is missing required entities (SoC, battery power, setpoint, work mode or Modbus control). Pick a device from the Anker X1 integration.

devices.name: Devices & sensors
devices.description: The X1 device and the core sensors the planner reads.

data_description.anker_device: Battery capacity and the control entities are re-derived automatically when this changes.
data_description.ent_price: Sensor reporting the current electricity import tariff in €/kWh. Drives all charge and export planning.
data_description.ent_export_price: Sensor with the feed-in price in €/kWh. Leave empty to mirror the import price (salderen).
data_description.ent_weather_forecast: Weather entity providing the hourly forecast used by the load model.
data_description.person_entities: Person entities whose home/away state feeds the load forecast model. Leave empty to disable the home-presence signal.

solar_forecast.name: Solar forecast
solar_forecast.description: Where PV production forecasts come from.

data_description.forecast_service: Pick one or more forecast integrations. Their PV sensors are added together (summed), so select multiple only when they cover different arrays — otherwise PV reads high. Leave empty to keep your current sensors.
data_description.ent_pv_today: Sensors with today's remaining PV production in kWh; multiple sensors are summed. Auto-derived from the selected forecast integrations.
data_description.ent_pv_tomorrow: Sensors with tomorrow's PV production in kWh; multiple sensors are summed. Auto-derived from the selected forecast integrations.
data_description.ent_pv_peak_today: Sensors with today's PV peak-power time, index-aligned with the PV energy sensors. Auto-derived; edit only if you know the alignment rules.
data_description.ent_pv_peak_tomorrow: Sensors with tomorrow's PV peak-power time, index-aligned with the PV energy sensors. Auto-derived; edit only if you know the alignment rules.

battery.name: Battery & charging
battery.description: State-of-charge margins, efficiencies and charge economics.

data_description.soc_floor: Soft minimum state of charge the planner protects. The firmware enforces a hard 5% floor below this. Default 5.
data_description.soc_target: State of charge the planner aims for in cheap hours. Default 97.
data_description.eta_charge: One-way AC→battery charging efficiency (0.5–1.0), used while measured efficiency is off. Default 0.92.
data_description.round_trip_eff: Charge+discharge round-trip efficiency (0.5–1.0) used to value stored energy. Default 0.85.
data_description.use_measured_eta: Derive the efficiency curve from recorded charge sessions instead of the static values above. Default off.
data_description.charge_margin_eur_per_kwh: Minimum saving a grid charge must clear before it is scheduled; stops overnight micro-charges. Default 0.
data_description.charge_trough_lookback_h: Hours of past prices the cheap-charge band remembers, so hours after the trough are not judged cheap. 0 disables. Default 8.

export.name: Export & arbitrage
export.description: Selling battery energy to the grid at price peaks.

data_description.enable_export: Allow the planner to sell battery energy to the grid at price peaks. Default on.
data_description.grid_export_limit_w: Maximum net export power your grid connection allows (fuse/contract limit — not a device property). Default 6000.
data_description.export_fee_eur_per_kwh: Feed-in fee your supplier subtracts per exported kWh. Default 0.02.
data_description.cycle_cost_eur_per_kwh: Battery wear cost per stored kWh; an export must beat this to be scheduled. Default 0.04.
data_description.reserve_anchor: How the overnight survival reserve is sized: ride-to-trough (self-scaling, default) or the legacy debit-to-trough estimate.
data_description.reserve_cheap_band: Fraction (0–1) above the forward trough price at which a later hour counts as cheap relief and ends the ride-out. Default 0.20.
data_description.export_dwell_min: Minimum minutes between export engage/disengage transitions. Default 15.
data_description.export_eps_lo_kwh: Surplus below which export disengages. Default 0.2.
data_description.export_eps_hi_kwh: Surplus above which export engages. Default 0.4.
data_description.export_peak_band_frac: Export only within this fraction below the horizon peak price (0.12 = within 12% of the peak). Default 0.12.
data_description.export_peak_lookback_h: Hours of recent past the peak reference remembers, so post-peak hours are not sold cheap. 0 disables. Default 4.
data_description.export_min_block_kwh: Contiguous export runs smaller than this are dropped from the plan. 0 disables. Default 0.5.
data_description.export_load_comp_factor: Fraction of live house load added to the export setpoint so the planned NET export reaches the grid. 1.0 = full compensation. Default 1.0.

price_anticipation.name: Price anticipation
price_anticipation.description: Estimating tomorrow-morning prices from price history.

data_description.price_history_days: Days of realized prices kept for the price prior. Default 8.
data_description.price_blend_weight_today: Weight of today's prices versus same-weekday-last-week in the morning estimate (0–1). Default 0.5.
data_description.anticipation_confidence_haircut: Discount applied to the estimated morning price before comparing with tonight (0–1). Default 0.15.
data_description.anticipation_margin_eur_per_kwh: The morning estimate must beat tonight's price by this much before charging is deferred. Default 0.02.

load_ml.name: Load model & ML
load_ml.description: House-load forecasting and the machine-learning engine.

data_description.use_learned_model: Forecast house load with the trained ML model instead of profile averages once enough data exists. Default on.
data_description.min_train_samples: Deprecated — no longer used. The learned load model activates automatically after 48 hourly rollups (~2 days of data).
data_description.load_adapt_fraction: How strongly the intraday corrector shifts the load forecast toward the last hours' residuals (0–1, 0 = off). Default 0.7.
data_description.load_adapt_window_h: Hours of recent residuals the intraday corrector averages. Default 5.
data_description.load_adapt_fade_h: Hours over which the intraday correction fades back to zero. Default 8.
data_description.addon_enabled: Offload ML training and forecasting to the Anker X1 Forecast add-on instead of running in-process. Default off.
data_description.addon_url: Base URL of the Anker X1 Forecast add-on. Default http://local-anker_x1_forecast:8099.
data_description.addon_timeout: HTTP timeout for add-on requests, 1–60 seconds. Default 5.

system.name: System
system.description: Slot resolution, data retention and drift correction.

data_description.slot_resolution: Planning slot length. Auto detects it from the price sensor's slot spacing. Default auto.
data_description.retention_days: Days of per-minute recorder samples kept in the local database. Default 90.
data_description.retention_hourly_days: Days of hourly rollups kept for the load model. Default 730.
data_description.soc_hedge_fraction: Fraction of measured SoC drift debited from the plan when live SoC runs below it (0 = off). Default 0.
data_description.soc_drift_deadband_kwh: SoC drift smaller than this is ignored. Default 0.3.
data_description.soc_drift_decay_halflife_h: Half-life for decaying accumulated drift (0 = no decay). Default 0.

error.anker_roles_missing: The selected device is missing required entities (SoC, battery power, setpoint, work mode or Modbus control). Pick a device from the Anker X1 integration.
error.soc_floor_above_target: SoC floor must be below SoC target.
